feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: COVID-19 vaccine study that was blocked from CDC journal is published elsewhere
entry.summary: A study on COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness has been published after being blocked from a government health journal. The study in by JAMA Network Open shows the vaccine is about 55% effective against hospitalizations and reduces emergency visits by 50%. The fin…

entry.title: A highly infectious disease has been found in Utah's wild rabbit population again
entry.summary: A highly infectious, often fatal viral disease affecting rabbits has been detected in Utah's wildlife for the first time in four years.

entry.title: Yes, You Can Fix Your Aging Neck
entry.summary: The face may get all the attention when it comes to cosmetic treatments, but the neck deserves love too. Novel technologies that go beyond creams deliver lifted, sculpted necks—without surgery.

entry.title: Mom's Confusion Dismissed as Menopause and Anxiety. Then She's Diagnosed with Incurable Brain Cancer
entry.summary: “It was completely harrowing," Jack Dilger said of his mother's diagnosis, which eventually led to 13 brain surgeries in two years

entry.title: Woman Donates $90K to YMCA Program for Alzheimer’s Patients and Their Caregivers in Honor of Late Husband
entry.summary: Sally Phillips said the community center provided physical and social opportunities for her husband Ken Titus after he was diagnosed with dementia

entry.title: ‘Frankenstein’ Rabbits With Tentacle-Like Growths Are Appearing Across the US
entry.summary: Shope papillomavirus causes the alarming horn-like growths on US rabbits. It poses no risk to humans, but pet rabbit owners should take precautions.

entry.title: CDC’s chief blocked a covid vaccine study. Now it’s in a top medical journal.
entry.summary: The study found that the vaccine reduced the risk of emergency department visits and hospitalizations among healthy adults by about half last winter.

entry.title: Millennials may not be aging as well as they think — study finds ’90s babies are aging faster, have higher cancer risk
entry.summary: A new study suggests that younger generations are aging faster and at higher risk of cancer than older generations when they were the same age.

entry.title: Experts say these cereals are linked to lowering your risk of a stroke
entry.summary: Strokes are considered one of the leading causes of death in America, according to the CDC

entry.title: Developmental issues in kids spike 62% when mom has key health issue
entry.summary: “Optimizing a woman’s heart health before and during the time of the pregnancy may be possibly one of the earliest opportunities that we have to influence lifelong health outcomes for both th…

entry.title: Popular pain supplement linked to faster Alzheimer’s decline — and death
entry.summary: While many turn to a popular supplement to ease joint pain, new research suggests they could actually speed up cognitive decline.

entry.title: Study Finds Many Older Adults Will Improve Over Time–Depending on Their Mindset
entry.summary: The improvements were not limited to a small group of exceptional individuals and, notably, were linked to an often overlooked factor.

entry.title: 9 Fruits Packed With Prebiotics That Support a Healthier Gut
entry.summary: Which fruits have the most prebiotics? Fruit rich in prebiotic fiber to feed good bacteria in the gut includes bananas, apples, blueberries and nectarines.

entry.title: Keep an eye on your vision health with TRICARE
entry.summary: About 11 million Americans over age 12 need vision correction, according to the CDC. Do you know if you’re one of them? Getting routine eye exams to monitor your eyesight can help detect and prevent

entry.title: I’m an oncologist — 5 cancer symptoms people dismiss as normal aging or stress
entry.summary: Many cancer symptoms overlap with changes people commonly associate with getting older, making treatable cancers more difficult to detect. Knowing which symptoms are often dismissed can help people…

entry.title: Your Taste For Onions May Reveal Something About Your Future Health
entry.summary: Diet and health are closely related, but the details can be fuzzy, with so many variables at play.

entry.title: We Asked a Doctor If Taking Magnesium Before Bed Can Improve Insulin Sensitivity
entry.summary: Magnesium could help improve insulin sensitivity, though you don't need to take it at a certain time of day to reap the benefits. You should speak with a healthcare before starting magnesium supplementation to make sure it's safe for you.

entry.title: Column | Want a younger brain? Exercise in your 40s may help.
entry.summary: Even if you’ve never worked out, starting in middle age — even with brisk walks — can have a bigger impact on your brain than you think.

entry.title: No. 1 Mistake People Make When Buying Walking Shoes That Harms Foot Health
entry.summary: In TODAY.com’s Expert Tip of the Day, a physical therapist reveals a common mistake people make when buying walking shoes that can harm foot health, and how to avoid it.

entry.title: The 5 mosquito-borne diseases you’re most likely to get in the U.S.
entry.summary: These diseases usually cause mild symptoms (or none at all), but they can have more serious complications in certain people.

entry.title: Want to age better? Researchers say 4-minute routine may help prevent dangerous falls
entry.summary: A Penn State clinical trial finds just four minutes of daily strength exercise can improve mobility, balance, and leg strength in older adults.

entry.title: 8 Probiotic Superfoods Recommended by a Nutritionist to Support Gut Health
entry.summary: A nutritionist and a registered dietitian share which foods help populate good bacteria in your gut.

entry.title: Shocking side effect of blood pressure medication sent woman to the ER
entry.summary: A swollen lip was the first sign of a life-threatening reaction to a medication she’d been on for 10 years.

entry.title: A Common Supplement Shows a Concerning Link to Heart Failure
entry.summary: One of the most popular supplements sold in the US could be having an unintended side effect, research suggests.

entry.title: This is how to wean off an SSRI, according to a psychiatrist
entry.summary: Here’s what SSRIs are and how to safely stop taking them, per expert medical advice.

entry.title: Tetanus cases are on the rise. A doctor explains what to know and how to protect yourself
entry.summary: Recent CDC reports found dozens of tetanus deaths since 2011 and four cases in children in 2024. CNN’s Dr. Leana Wen explains how people can protect themselves.

entry.title: 3 People Have Gotten Cancer-Detecting Implants in Their Brains
entry.summary: Coherence Neuro has started testing a brain-computer interface that could one day use electrical stimulation to prevent tumors from growing.

entry.title: One Protein May Prevent Brain Process Linked to Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s
entry.summary: What matters isn’t the condensate—it’s whether tubulin is present, an associate professor told Newsweek.

entry.title: A Son Removes His Credit Card From His Mother’s DoorDash Account After Her Cancer Progresses, Leading To Family Disagreement Over Her Care
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Bird Flu Has Reached The Last Free Continent. Can We Stop Its Spread?
entry.summary: On a remote beach near Esperance, Western Australia, two sick seabirds have brought the bird flu crisis to Australia.

entry.title: This emerging treatment is helping people avoid knee replacement surgery
entry.summary: A minimally invasive treatment called GAE is helping people with chronic knee pain get back to gardening, cycling, and other activities without undergoing knee replacement surgery. Early studies suggest the procedure can provide years of relief by reducing in…

entry.title: Thousands of men with prostate cancer will now be offered high-powered radiotherapy on the NHS
entry.summary: Thousands of men with prostate cancer will now be offered high-powered radiotherapy on the NHS

entry.title: Raccoon tests positive for rabies after dog attack in backyard of N.J. home
entry.summary: A rabid raccoon killed by dogs in Far Hills prompts officials to urge pet vaccination and public caution.

entry.title: Strength Training Is Linked to Longevity, 30-Year Study Finds
entry.summary: Strength training has long been seen as something you mainly do to build muscle or look good.

entry.title: COVID Vaccine Linked to New Side Effect, Especially in Older Adults
entry.summary: The COVID vaccine was linked to a new side effect for older adults.

entry.title: Doctors Are Sharing The Wildly Concerning Trends That Are Landing People In The Hospital
entry.summary: "RN here, taking care of a patient who stopped taking her blood pressure meds and started taking TikTok supplements instead. Her 12-year-old children found her down after a cardiac arrest. She's now anoxic, and the family is deciding if they want to keep her …

entry.title: This Drink Could Help Lower Your Cholesterol After Menopause, New Study Says
entry.summary: Green tea may help lower cholesterol after menopause, according to recent research. Discover findings on green tea and metabolic health for women.

entry.title: ‘Surviving and Thriving at Altitude’: Summit doctor publishes book giving tips on high elevation health in Colorado’s mountains
entry.summary: Physician Christine Ebert-Santos, whose family-practice clinic sits over 9,000 feet above sea level, is no stranger to treating patients reacting poorly to elevation. She says her patients aren't all visitors from sea level or the front range experiencing hi…

entry.title: Tick that spreads meat allergy, flu-like symptoms has reached 3 Connecticut counties
entry.summary: NEW HAVEN, Conn. (WTNH) — Experts say two invasive tick species have made their way to Connecticut, particularly in three coastal counties. Lone star ticks and Asian longhorned ticks are not native to the nutmeg state. Last week, the species had established p…

entry.title: Millions of Metformin drug users issued serious four day warning
entry.summary: The warning is due to the drug making users more susceptible to one thing

entry.title: These are the best cereals to pick for lowering stroke risk, experts say
entry.summary: Strokes are considered one of the leading causes of death in America, according to the CDC

entry.title: Utah measles outbreak among largest, ‘definitely the longest,” health official says
entry.summary: How many people in Utah got measles in this outbreak? What causes measles? Is the measles outbreak in Utah over? What are measles symptoms?

entry.title: As Ebola cases hit 1,000, almost 3 million children and adolescents face rising risks in eastern DR Congo
entry.summary: Ebola has left over 130 children orphaned in Ituri, driving urgent need for protection and care

entry.title: One underlying cause of inflammatory bowel disease pinpointed in new study
entry.summary: Autoantibodies may be disabling one of the body's anti-inflammatory brakes in some IBD patients, a new study finds.

entry.title: Quarantine comes to an end for the last of the hantavirus ship passengers in Nebraska
entry.summary: The last eight Americans who endured 42 days in a specialized hospital quarantine unit after exposure to an unusual hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship that killed three people have left the Nebraska facility. U.S. Department of Health and Human Services off…

entry.title: 3 Health Benefits of Metformin Beyond Blood Sugar Control
entry.summary: Metformin is commonly prescribed for type 2 diabetes, but it may also offer additional benefits, including effects on weight, PCOS, and heart health.

entry.title: Granola: Why This Popular Breakfast Could Be Raising Your Blood Pressure
entry.summary: It’s really popular, too.

entry.title: 6 health issues that may shrink your testicles — including a condition more common on the left side
entry.summary: Dr. Alexander Pastuszak, a urologist and male reproductive medicine specialist, spoke to The Post about what could shrink the jewels.

entry.title: Whole Grain Pasta vs. Regular Pasta: Which Is Better for Blood Sugar?
entry.summary: If you're deciding between whole grain or regular pasta for your next meal, here's how they stack up for blood sugar effects.

entry.title: I think I might have Covid. (updated x 2)
entry.summary: No, not Morse. Me. I was fine last night. Woke up this morning to a godawful sore throat and aching like I’d been beaten up in Tinyhands McFelony’s shitty cage match. Took some Tylenol and powered…
